title: tylophora ovata
intro: Tylophora ovata, also known as Indian ipecac or tylophora, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. It is a perennial climber with medicinal properties, particularly its roots and leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Tylophora ovata include its anti-inflammatory, antitussive, and expectorant properties, which provide health benefits such as relief from respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Tylophora ovata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, making it a popular remedy in Ayurvedic and traditional medicine.
